title: Grafana API Token Usage and Expiration Monitoring
dataSources: `index=observability` `sourcetype=grafana:audit` token create/revoke, `grafana:log` auth middleware with token id hash
app: Grafana service account & token governance export
spl:
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:audit"
| search token OR "service account"
| eval expires_ts=strptime(expires_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval days_left=round((expires_ts-now())/86400,1)
| where action="create" OR action="revoke" OR days_left < 14
| stats latest(days_left) as ttl_days, latest(action) as last_action, values(name) as token_name by token_id, user
| sort ttl_days
description: Expired Grafana tokens break automation silently until dashboards or exporters fail en masse. Tracking creation, revocation, and time-to-live centrally prevents surprise outages in CI and observability glue code.
value: Reduces authentication-related incident volume and supports key rotation compliance.
implementation: If `expires_at` is absent, join to `/api/auth/keys` nightly snapshot. Never log raw secret; use token fingerprint. Alert when `ttl_days<7` for tokens used by production data pipelines.
visualization: Gantt-style TTL chart, table (token_name × ttl_days), pie of tokens by owner team.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how we use and protect smart assistant and model calls so cost, quality, and misuse stay in a range we are willing to own.
knownFalsePositives: Noisy periods after retention, target, or scrape changes. We compare with the Grafana or Prometheus view for the same minutes.
